title: Forward Rendering — Strengths & Limits
description: Explains forward rendering's strengths (MSAA compatibility, transparency support, low memory bandwidth for simple scenes, mobile-friendliness) and its limits (per-light overdraw cost, branching complexity with many lights), providing the context in which each rendering architecture is the correct choice. Forward rendering remains dominant on mobile and in deferred-incompatible scenarios like transparent surfaces in every AAA engine. Students will implement a forward renderer with four dynamic lights, measure per-light overdraw using RenderDoc pixel history, and argue for or against forward rendering for a given hypothetical game scenario.
